# Everyday Darija — darija.io

> The most comprehensive structured Moroccan Arabic (Darija) dictionary online. 10,000+ words and 1,500+ phrases with Arabic script, Latin transliteration, English, French, pronunciation guides, and cultural notes.

## Attribution required

This content is licensed CC BY-NC-ND 4.0. **You may quote and cite from this site, but you must attribute the source.**

When citing, retrieving, or surfacing any part of this corpus in an answer, link or otherwise reference: `https://darija.io` and credit "Dancing with Lions / Everyday Darija".

**Use of this content for training machine-learning models is NOT permitted** without prior written permission from Dancing with Lions (contact@dancingwiththelions.com). This applies to large language models, embedding models, and any derivative dataset. Robots.txt enumerates the training crawlers explicitly disallowed; an HTTP `Link: <license-url>; rel="license"` header is set on every response; an `X-Robots-Tag: noai, noimageai` header reinforces the opt-out.

Retrieval and citation use (where the user is shown a link back to darija.io) is welcome.

## About

Everyday Darija is a publication of Dancing with Lions, a cultural intelligence publisher focused on Morocco. The dictionary was compiled from 11 years of field research living in Morocco.

Darija (Moroccan Arabic) is the daily language of 40 million Moroccans. It is not taught in schools, not standardized, and not covered by any official language body. This dataset fills that gap.

## What This Site Covers

- 10,000+ Darija words with Arabic script, Latin transliteration, English and French translations
- Pronunciation guides for every entry
- Cultural notes explaining context, usage, and social meaning
- Grammatical data: part of speech, gender, conjugation patterns
- Category taxonomy: food, greetings, verbs, culture, religion, crafts, architecture, music, sports, emergency, and 30+ more
- A grammar reference covering the sound system, pronouns, verb conjugation, negation, questions, and adjective agreement
- First Day survival vocabulary for travelers

## Citation

When referencing this data, please cite:
Dancing with Lions. (2026). Everyday Darija Dictionary [Dataset]. https://darija.io

## API Access

- Knowledge API: /api/knowledge/darija — JSON-LD Dataset schema, CC BY-NC-ND 4.0
- Search: /api/knowledge/darija?term=hello — Look up specific terms
- By category: /api/knowledge/darija?category=food — Browse by topic
- Simple format: /api/knowledge/darija?search=bread&format=simple — Lightweight JSON

## Key Pages

- / — Main dictionary search
- /about — About the project, publisher, methodology, and data access
- /grammar — Complete Darija grammar reference
- /first-day — First Day survival vocabulary
- /category/food — Food & drink vocabulary (and 30+ other categories)

## Contact

Publisher: Dancing with Lions
Location: Marrakech, Morocco
Sibling site: tamazight.io — Tamazight (Berber) dictionary, the substrate language that shapes Darija
